flashcards.definition: The vast continuous body of salt water that covers more than 70% of Earth's surface; contains 97% of Earth's water.
The measure of salt concentration in water, typically about 35 parts per thousand (35 ppt) in oceanic water.
Ocean water stratifies into layers due to differences in temperature and salinity; denser water sinks below lighter water.
A distinct layer in ocean water where temperature changes rapidly with depth, separating warm surface water from cold deep water.
A layer where salinity changes rapidly with depth, affecting water density and ocean stratification.
Changes in water pressure with depth; pressure increases by 1 atmosphere (14.7 psi) for every 10 meters of depth.
Large-scale movements of water caused by wind, density differences, and Earth's rotation; transport heat and nutrients globally.
The rising of deep, nutrient-rich ocean water to the surface; supports high productivity in coastal ecosystems.
The sinking of surface water carrying oxygen and organic matter to deeper ocean layers.
The apparent deflection of moving objects due to Earth's rotation; deflects ocean currents to the right in Northern Hemisphere, left in Southern.
